NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. On a stringed instrument. A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, O God; Attend to my prayer.
NKJV.2: From the end of the earth I will cry to You, When my heart is overwhelmed; Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
NKJV.3: For You have been a shelter for me,  A strong tower from the enemy.
NKJV.4: I will abide in Your tabernacle forever; I will trust in the shelter of Your wings. Selah
NKJV.5: For You, O God, have heard my vows; You have given me the heritage of those who fear Your name.
NKJV.6: You will prolong the king’s life, His years as many generations.
NKJV.7: He shall abide before God forever. Oh, prepare mercy and truth, which may preserve him!
NKJV.8: So I will sing praise to Your name forever, That I may daily perform my vows.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Neginah<S>5058</S>, A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>. Hear<S>8085</S> my cry<S>7440</S>, O God<S>430</S>; attend<S>7181</S> unto my prayer<S>8605</S>.
KJV.2: From the end<S>7097</S> of the earth<S>776</S> will I cry<S>7121</S> unto thee, when my heart<S>3820</S> is overwhelmed<S>5848</S>: lead<S>5148</S> me to the rock<S>6697</S> that is higher<S>7311</S> than I.
KJV.3: For thou hast been a shelter<S>4268</S> for me, and a strong<S>5797</S> tower<S>4026</S> from<S>6440</S> the enemy<S>341</S>.
KJV.4: I will abide<S>1481</S> in thy tabernacle<S>168</S> for ever<S>5769</S>: I will trust<S>2620</S> in the covert<S>5643</S> of thy wings<S>3671</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>trust: or, make my refuge</sup>

KJV.6: Thou wilt prolong<S>3254</S> the king's<S>4428</S> life<S>3117</S> <S>3117</S>: and his years<S>8141</S> as many<S>1755</S> generations<S>1755</S>. <sup>Thou: Heb. Thou shalt add days to the days of the king</sup> <sup>as: Heb. as generation and generation</sup>
KJV.7: He shall abide<S>3427</S> before<S>6440</S> God<S>430</S> for ever<S>5769</S>: O prepare<S>4487</S> mercy<S>2617</S> and truth<S>571</S>, which may preserve<S>5341</S> him.
KJV.8: So will I sing<S>2167</S> praise unto thy name<S>8034</S> for ever<S>5703</S>, that I may daily<S>3117</S> <S>3117</S> perform<S>7999</S> my vows<S>5088</S>.

ESV.1: Hear my cry, O God, listen to my prayer;
ESV.2: from the end of the earth I call to you when my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I,
ESV.3: for you have been my refuge, a strong tower against the enemy.
ESV.4: Let me dwell in your tent forever! Let me take refuge under the shelter of your wings! Selah
ESV.5: For you, O God, have heard my vows; you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
ESV.6: Prolong the life of the king; may his years endure to all generations!
ESV.7: May he be enthroned forever before God; appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to watch over him!
ESV.8: So will I ever sing praises to your name, as I perform my vows day after day.

NASB.1: For the choir director; on a stringed instrument. [A Psalm] of David. Hear my cry, O God; Give heed to my prayer.
NASB.2: From the end of the earth I call to You when my heart is faint; Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
NASB.3: For You have been a refuge for me, A tower of strength against the enemy.
NASB.4: Let me dwell in Your tent forever; Let me take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah.
NASB.5: For You have heard my vows, O God; You have given [me] the inheritance of those who fear Your name.
NASB.6: You will prolong the king's life; His years will be as many generations.
NASB.7: He will abide before God forever; Appoint lovingkindness and truth that they may preserve him.
NASB.8: So I will sing praise to Your name forever, That I may pay my vows day by day.

NLT.1:
O God, listen to my cry!
Hear my prayer!
NLT.2:
From the ends of the earth,
I cry to you for help
when my heart is overwhelmed.
Lead me to the towering rock of safety,
NLT.3:
for you are my safe refuge,
a fortress where my enemies cannot reach me.
NLT.4:
Let me live forever in your sanctuary,
safe beneath the shelter of your wings! Interlude
NLT.5:
For you have heard my vows, O God.
You have given me an inheritance reserved for those who fear your name.
NLT.6:
Add many years to the life of the king!
May his years span the generations!
NLT.7:
May he reign under God’s protection forever.
May your unfailing love and faithfulness watch over him.
NLT.8:
Then I will sing praises to your name forever
as I fulfill my vows each day.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. For a stringed instrument. By David. Hear my cry, God. Listen to my prayer.
WEB.2: From the end of the earth, I will call to you, when my heart is overwhelmed. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
WEB.3: For you have been a refuge for me, a strong tower from the enemy.
WEB.4: I will dwell in your tent forever. I will take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah.
WEB.5: For you, God, have heard my vows. You have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
WEB.6: You will prolong the king’s life; his years shall be for generations.
WEB.7: He shall be enthroned in God’s presence forever. Appoint your loving kindness and truth, that they may preserve him.
WEB.8: So I will sing praise to your name forever, that I may fulfill my vows daily.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: on stringed instruments. Of David. [1] God, hear my cry;
pay attention to my prayer. ⓚ
CSB17.2:
I call to you from the ends of the earth
when my heart is without strength. ⓛ
Lead me to a rock that is high above me, ⓜ
CSB17.3:
for you have been a refuge for me,
a strong tower ⓝ in the face of the enemy.
CSB17.4:
I will dwell in your tent forever
and take refuge under the shelter of your wings. ⓐ Selah
CSB17.5:
God, you have heard my vows; ⓑ
you have given a heritage
to those who fear your name. ⓒ
CSB17.6:
Add days to the king’s life;
may his years span many generations. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
May he sit enthroned before God forever. ⓔ
Appoint faithful love and truth to guard him. ⓕ
CSB17.8:
Then I will continually sing of your name, ⓖ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. A Psalm of David. Hear my cry, O God;
Listen to my prayer.
AMP.2:
From the end of the earth I call to You, when my heart is overwhelmed and weak;
Lead me to the rock that is higher than I [a rock that is too high to reach without Your help].
AMP.3:
For You have been a shelter and a refuge for me,
A strong tower against the enemy.
AMP.4:
Let me dwell in Your tent forever;
Let me take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah.
AMP.5:
For You have heard my vows, O God;
You have given me the inheritance of those who fear Your name [with reverence].
AMP.6:
You will prolong the king's life [adding days upon days];
His years will be like many generations.
AMP.7:
He will sit enthroned forever before [the face of] God;
Appoint lovingkindness and truth to watch over and preserve him.
AMP.8:
So I will sing praise to Your name forever,
Paying my vows day by day.

MSG.1:
God, listen to me shout,
bend an ear to my prayer.
MSG.2:
When I'm far from anywhere,
down to my last gasp,
I call out, "Guide me
up High Rock Mountain!"
MSG.3:
You've always given me breathing room,
a place to get away from it all,
MSG.4:
A lifetime pass to your safe-house,
an open invitation as your guest.
MSG.5:
You've always taken me seriously, God,
made me welcome among those who know and love you.
MSG.6:
Let the days of the king add up
to years and years of good rule.
MSG.7:
Set his throne in the full light of God;
post Steady Love and Good Faith as lookouts,
MSG.8:
And I'll be the poet who sings your glory—
and live what I sing every day.

NIV.1:
Psalm 61
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. Of David.
Hear my cry, O God;
listen to my prayer.
NIV.2:
From the ends of the earth I call to you,
I call as my heart grows faint;
lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
NIV.3:
For you have been my refuge,
a strong tower against the foe.
NIV.4:
I long to dwell in your tent forever
and take refuge in the shelter of your wings. Selah
NIV.5:
For you have heard my vows, O God;
you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
NIV.6:
Increase the days of the king’s life,
his years for many generations.
NIV.7:
May he be enthroned in God’s presence forever;
appoint your love and faithfulness to protect him.
NIV.8:
Then will I ever sing praise to your name
and fulfill my vows day after day.

NRSVCE.1:
Hear my cry, O God;
listen to my prayer.
NRSVCE.2:
From the end of the earth I call to you,
when my heart is faint.
Lead me to the rock
that is higher than I;
NRSVCE.3:
for you are my refuge,
a strong tower against the enemy.
NRSVCE.4:
Let me abide in your tent forever,
find refuge under the shelter of your wings. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
For you, O God, have heard my vows;
you have given me the heritage of those who fear your name.
NRSVCE.6:
Prolong the life of the king;
may his years endure to all generations!
NRSVCE.7:
May he be enthroned forever before God;
appoint steadfast love and faithfulness to watch over him!
NRSVCE.8:
So I will always sing praises to your name,
as I pay my vows day after day.

NET.1: O God, hear my cry for help! Pay attention to my prayer!
NET.2: From the most remote place on earth I call out to you in my despair. Lead me up to an inaccessible rocky summit!
NET.3: Indeed, you are my shelter, a strong tower that protects me from the enemy.
NET.4: I will be a permanent guest in your home; I will find shelter in the protection of your wings. (Selah)
NET.5: For you, O God, hear my vows; you grant me the reward that belongs to your loyal followers.
NET.6: Give the king long life! Make his lifetime span several generations!
NET.7: May he reign forever before God! Decree that your loyal love and faithfulness should protect him.
NET.8: Then I will sing praises to your name continually, as I fulfill my vows day after day.
